title: How to vibe code with AI tools like GitHub Copilot, Cursor AI, and Loveable | Vinay C posted on the topic | LinkedIn
author: Vinay C
text:
If you’ve never tried “Vibe Coding”, you are living in stoneage! It’s not about just writing code anymore. It’s about collaborating with AI tools that feel like teammates. Here are 3 tools every engineer should try at least once: 1. GitHub Copilot Agents – Not just autocomplete. These agents think with you. 2. Cursor AI – (My favourite) The AI-first IDE that rewires how you debug, refactor, and explore codebases. 3. Loveable – Your personal AI pair programmer that’s surprisingly intuitive and… well, loveable. 😉 Whether you’re building solo or scaling systems, these tools shift you into a new flow state — what I call vibe coding. Curious to hear — have you tried any of these? What’s your experience? #AI #DeveloperTools #VibeCoding #Copilot #CursorAI #Loveable #Productivity
How to vibe code with AI tools like GitHub Copilot, Cursor AI, and Loveable

🚀 Vibe Coding — The Future of Building Software by Talking to AI Ever heard of Vibe Coding? 💡 It’s the new wave of development where you describe what you want in plain language — and AI writes the code for you. Coined by Andrej Karpathy, vibe coding shifts your role from coder to creator — focusing on ideas, logic, and flow instead of syntax. 🧠 Why it’s trending: Build prototypes in minutes ⚡ No need to handle every line of code Perfect for creative problem-solving 🛠️ Popular tools: Replit, Cursor, Bolt.new, v0 (by Vercel), Lovable, and GitHub Copilot. Yes, it’s fast — but remember, review and testing still matter. AI can code the “what,” but you define the “why.” #VibeCoding #AI #Developers #Innovation #FutureOfWork #Programming

𝗜𝘀 𝗰𝗼𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗱𝗲𝗮𝗱? With AI tools like GitHub Copilot spitting out full apps in minutes, it’s a fair question. But here’s the reality after 𝟭𝟱+ 𝘆𝗲𝗮𝗿𝘀 𝗶𝗻 𝘁𝗲𝗰𝗵: AI handles the 𝟴𝟬% of code we hate—boilerplate, CRUD, scaffolding. The last 𝟮𝟬%—the complex logic, scaling, and debugging at 3 AM—that still needs a human brain. Yes, AI will soon write 𝟮𝟬–𝟰𝟬% of all code. But the total amount of software being built is set to grow by 𝟭𝟬𝘅. That means more opportunities for developers, not fewer. It’s like Excel and accountants: the tool didn’t replace them—it amplified them. Same story here. The winners won’t be the ones who crank out the most lines of code. They’ll be the ones who can 𝗼𝗿𝗰𝗵𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲 𝗔𝗜 systems to build things that matter. Don’t fight the wave—learn to surf it. What do you think: is AI killing coding, or making it more powerful than ever? #GenerativeAI #SoftwareDevelopment #FutureOfWork #AIEngineering #TechLeadership #ArtificialIntelligence #Coding

Most people think AI in coding is just autocomplete. But the truth? It’s becoming much more than that. Here are the 5 coding agents I actually use (and why they’ve earned their spot): 1. GitHub Copilot → everyday speed. It’s my extra pair of hands in VS Code. 2. Cursor → project-level thinking. It doesn’t just finish lines, it understands repos. 3. Devin → the one I’m watching closely. It feels like a glimpse of the future. 4. Replit Agent → quick prototypes, browser-based. Perfect for testing ideas without setup. 5. OpenAI o1 → my go-to when I hit a wall in debugging or logic-heavy tasks. I’ve tried dozens of tools. These five are the ones that actually stay open on my screen. AI isn’t replacing developers but developers who learn to use AI will replace those who don’t. #AI #Coding #FutureOfWork #SoftwareEngineering #software #developer

Is the future of coding about writing less and building more? It's a question on every developer's mind. The pressure to ship faster without sacrificing quality or budget is immense. We're seeing a fundamental shift in the development lifecycle, and the tools we use are at the heart of this transformation. One tool making serious waves is Claude Haiku 4.5, which just had a massive day on Product Hunt. Here’s why it’s catching everyone's attention: It's not just another AI model; it's a purpose-built coding engine designed for speed and efficiency. Think of it as a super-powered pair programmer that never needs a coffee break. ☕ What’s under the hood? ⚡️ Blazing-fast code generation for rapid prototyping and iteration. 💰 Unmatched affordability, making powerful AI accessible to solo devs and large teams alike. 🧠 An innovative model that streamlines complex coding tasks, turning ideas into reality quicker than ever. The buzz from the developer community is real. When a tool can demonstrably cut down development time and costs, it's more than just an innovation—it's a competitive advantage. This isn't just about AI doing the work; it's about augmenting human creativity. What are your thoughts on AI's growing role in software development? #AI #ClaudeHaiku #DeveloperTools #ProductHunt #FutureOfCode #ArtificialIntelligence #SoftwareDevelopment #Coding

Is your AI coding assistant secretly mortgaging your project's future? We're all celebrating the incredible productivity gains from tools like GitHub Copilot. They are phenomenal for scaffolding code, writing boilerplate, and solving isolated problems in seconds. But we're not talking enough about the hidden technical debt they are accumulating in our codebases. This isn't about the occasional buggy snippet; it's something far more subtle. Recently, a colleague spent the better part of a day debugging a critical performance issue. The root cause was a clever, complex function generated by an AI assistant months ago. It worked perfectly for the initial use case, but nobody on the team *truly* understood its inner logic or its edge-case behaviors. The few minutes saved in writing that function were paid back tenfold in debugging hours. This is the new face of technical debt: Lack of Ownership: Code that "just appears" doesn't foster the same deep understanding as code that is meticulously thought through. It becomes a foreign object in your own system, making future maintenance a nightmare. Architectural Drift: AI assistants are tactical, not strategic. They don't know your long-term architectural vision. Over time, dozens of these small, isolated "solutions" can lead to an inconsistent, patched-together system that resists scaling. The Understanding Gap: Over-reliance on AI can prevent developers, especially junior ones, from learning the 'why' behind the 'how'. They master prompting instead of mastering first-principles problem-solving. This isn't a call to abandon these game-changing tools. It's a call for a new kind of engineering discipline. We must treat AI-generated code as a suggestion from a brilliant but context-blind partner. Review it. Refactor it. And most importantly, ensure you understand it completely before you commit it. The goal is to augment our intelligence, not replace our diligence. How is your team creating new processes or guardrails to manage the unique challenges of AI-assisted development? #AI #TechnicalDebt #SoftwareDevelopment #DeveloperTools #EngineeringLeadership

Here's the uncomfortable truth about AI coding assistants that nobody's saying out loud: They're not replacing developers. They're exposing who was just memorizing Stack Overflow answers. A developer recently tracked 30 days using GitHub Copilot exclusively. The results? Wild. ⚡ Code output: Up 2x 🎯 Time spent thinking: Up 75% ⏰ Time writing boilerplate: Down 80% But here's the twist The hardest bugs? Still required human thinking. The best architecture? Still needed experience. The cleanest code? Still demanded judgment. AI handled the "what" brilliantly. Developers still own the "why." The difference between a senior dev and a junior dev isn't typing speed anymore. It's knowing when to accept the AI suggestion and when to delete it. The developers panicking about AI are the ones who thought coding was about syntax. The developers thriving with AI are the ones who always knew it was about problem-solving. Tools change. Problems don't. Master the problems, and the tools become irrelevant. What's your take? Using AI assistants or avoiding them? #SoftwareDevelopment #AI #GitHubCopilot #Programming #TechTrends #DeveloperLife #Coding #Innovation

💡 AI as Your Coding Partner: The Rise of “Vibe Coding” Some days, I open VS Code not to build something massive — but just to vibe. You know that flow state where you’re testing an idea, prompting your AI assistant, and suddenly an hour feels like five minutes? That’s vibe coding. You’re not forcing productivity — you’re collaborating with AI to explore, tinker, and learn. Here’s how I use AI daily as a developer: ⚙️ ChatGPT / Claude / Gemini → to brainstorm architecture, refactor logic, or debug when my brain’s foggy. 📘 GitHub Copilot → for instant code context + smart auto-completion (like magic when working with TypeScript). 🎨 Cursor / Windsurf IDEs → for conversational coding that feels intuitive. 💬 Perplexity → to deep-dive docs when StackOverflow doesn’t cut it. The pros? 🚀 Faster feedback loops 🎯 Less burnout from “blank screen” moments 🧠 More time for creative logic The cons? 🤖 Over-reliance can dull your raw problem-solving 🧩 Harder to trace why something works if you didn’t build it step-by-step Still, if you balance it right — AI becomes your thinking amplifier, not a replacement. ✨ My routine now is: morning coffee, lo-fi beats, AI chat open on one side, and an empty file ready for ideas. No pressure. Just flow. 👉 How do you use AI in your daily coding routine? Are you team “Copilot all the way” or “no AI in my zone”? #vibecode #github #cursor #kiro #copilot #coding #developers

🤖 My AI coding strategy - What's yours? I currently use GitHub Copilot, Claude, and Cursor in my daily workflow, and I've developed an approach that might seem unconventional: **My current strategy:** When facing a new issue or feature, I consult all three AIs to get the best possible approach. I compare their suggestions, combine the best ideas, and then move forward with implementation. The reasoning: each AI has different strengths, and by triangulating their responses, I feel I'm making more informed architectural decisions. **But I'm questioning this approach.** While it helps me feel confident about the solution, I wonder if: • I'm spending too much time comparing responses • There's decision fatigue from having too many options • Other developers have found more efficient workflows **So I want to ask the community:** 📌 How do you use AI tools in your development workflow? 📌 Do you use multiple AIs or stick to one? 📌 How do you decide which tool to use for what task? 📌 What's your framework for: architecture design, debugging, refactoring, boilerplate code, and code reviews? I'm particularly curious about: • Your "rules of thumb" for tool selection • Any anti-patterns you've discovered • Time investment vs. productivity gains • How you avoid context-switching overhead **Have you found a system that works better than consulting multiple AIs?** Share your strategy in the comments 👇 #SoftwareDevelopment #AI #AIAssistedCoding #DeveloperProductivity #GitHubCopilot #Claude #Cursor #CodingWorkflow

🚀 Meet Cursor: The AI-Powered IDE That's Revolutionizing How We Code! Imagine having an AI pair programmer that understands your entire codebase, predicts your next move, and writes code faster than you can think. That's Cursor - the most popular AI-powered IDE of 2025! ✨ What makes Cursor a game-changer: • Built on VS Code with deep project understanding • Context-aware code suggestions that actually make sense • File-aware AI that knows your entire codebase • Seamless integration with existing workflows • Generous free tier for developers 🎯 Key use cases transforming development: • Lightning-fast prototyping and MVP creation • Complex refactoring made simple • Intelligent debugging and error resolution • Documentation generation on autopilot • Learning new frameworks through AI guidance 💡 Personal insight: We're witnessing the democratization of software development. Tools like Cursor aren't just making experienced developers more productive - they're lowering the barrier for newcomers to build incredible things. The future of coding is collaborative, intelligent, and accessible to all. The question isn't whether AI will change development - it's how quickly we'll adapt to this new paradigm. 🤔 What's your experience with AI-powered development tools? Are you team Cursor, or have you found another AI coding companion that's transformed your workflow? #AITools #ArtificialIntelligence #MachineLearning #TechTrends #Productivity #StartupTools #Innovation #LinkedInCreator #DevTools #AIForDevelopers

Vibe coding may or may not solve the hard problems. And that’s exactly why it’s changing the profession of IT. Most of the code I "write" today is AI-generated. Not because I’m lazy — but because so many tasks used to fall below the threshold of “worth building.” Little automations. Closing gaps. One-off data transforms. These weren’t “engineering” problems. They were manual-work-forever problems. Too small to automate, big enough to go on people's nerves and cause occasional issues. But now? I can prompt a model and get a working result in an hour or two. Not perfect code. Not genius code. But good enough — and that changes the economics. Maintenance? — If "writing" the code takes an hour, maintenance disappears. "Rewrite" if needed. This is where I see the niche for vibe coding: - Not solving the hardest problems. - Just solving the easy ones — at scale. That shift alone will transform how (and what) we build in the next decade. How do you use AI for coding? #AI #SoftwareDevelopment #Coding #Automation #VibeCoding #Productivity #LLMs
